# National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health (NCCIH)

The National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health (NCCIH) conducts and supports research and provides information about complementary health products and practices. It is part of the National Institutes of Health.

--- SOURCE 3: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health - Wikipedia ---
URL: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Center_for_Complementary_and_Integrative_Health

SUMMARY:
The **National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health** (**NCCIH**) is a United States government agency which explores complementary and alternative medicine (CAM). The OAM was expanded from an office into a center and renamed the National Center for Complementary and Alternative Medicine (NCCAM) in October 1998. Harkin responded by elevating OAM into an independent NIH "center", just short of being its own "institute", and renamed it the National Center for Complementary and Alternative Medicine (NCCAM). The NIH has also conducted research in alternative medicine at the National Cancer Institute by the Office of Cancer Complementary and Alternative Medicine which, in 2009, had the same $122 million budget as NCCIH. **^**"Josephine Briggs, M.D., Named Director of NIH's National Center for Complementary and Alternative Medicine".

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

US government agency funding alternative medicine research

![Image 4](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/9/98/Ambox_current_red.svg/60px-Ambox_current_red.svg.png)This article's **[factual accuracy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia:Accuracy_dispute "Wikipedia:Accuracy dispute") may be compromised due to out-of-date information**. Please help update this article to reflect recent events or newly available information._(August 2023)_

National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health[![Image 5](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fa/NIH-NCCIH-logo.svg/250px-NIH-NCCIH-logo.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:NIH-NCCIH-logo.svg)
Abbreviation NCCIH
Formation 1991
Type[Governmental organization](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Governmental_organization "Governmental organization")
Headquarters[Bethesda, Maryland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bethesda,_Maryland "Bethesda, Maryland")
Location*   United States
Official language English
Director[Helene Langevin](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Helene_Langevin "Helene Langevin"), MD
Parent organization[National Institutes of Health](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Institutes_of_Health "National Institutes of Health")
Affiliations[United States Public Health Service](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_States_Public_Health_Service "United States Public Health Service")
Website[nccih.nih.gov](http://nccih.nih.gov/)
Formerly called National Center for Complementary and Alternative Medicine (NCCAM)

Office of Alternative Medicine (OAM)

Part of [a series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Category:Alternative_medicine "Category:Alternative medicine") on
[Alternative medicine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alternative_medicine "Alternative medicine")
[![Image 6](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6c/Outline-body-aura.svg/60px-Outline-body-aura.svg.png)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Outline-body-aura.svg)

enriched_points.point_title: National Center for Complementary and Integrative Health
enriched_points.expanded_content: The NCCIH, part of the NIH, researches and disseminates information about complementary health products and practices, focusing on evidence-based integrative medicine.
Publishing in peer-reviewed journals ensures the credibility and rigor of research findings, aiding healthcare professionals in making informed decisions regarding integrative medicine practices.

Collaboration with healthcare institutions facilitates the integration of complementary and alternative medicine into clinical practice, promoting holistic patient care and comprehensive health strategies.
